title: rhododendron arboreum subsp. windsorii
intro: Rhododendron arboreum subsp. windsorii, commonly known as the Windsor rhododendron, is a subspecies of flowering plant that is native to the Himalayas in India and Nepal. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, where its leaves and flowers are valued for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The Windsor rhododendron is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help relieve congestion. The leaves and flowers of this plant can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
